risks: No monitoring or diagnostics resources (Log Analytics workspace, Application Insights) are included — operational visibility will be limited
No managed identity configuration on VMSS instances — workloads will lack secure credential-free access to Azure services
SQL Managed Instance is not configured with zone redundancy — a single-zone failure could impact the database tier
No Key Vault resource for SQL MI administrator credentials — the administratorLogin password has no secure storage mechanism in the plan
Application-tier VMSS lacks autoscale settings — it cannot elastically respond to load changes unlike the presentation tier
Load Balancer health probe is defined but not explicitly referenced in loadBalancingRules — traffic may not be routed based on health status
No WAF or L7 protection — the Standard Load Balancer is L4-only with no SSL offload or application-layer security
correctionsRecommended: Add a Log Analytics workspace and configure diagnostic settings for VMSS, Load Balancer, NSGs, and SQL MI to enable operational monitoring
Add a Key Vault resource and store the SQL MI administrator password as a secret rather than inline
Assign system-assigned managed identities to both VMSS instances for secure access to Azure resources
Add an autoscale settings resource (Microsoft.Insights/autoscaleSettings) targeting vmss-application-prod-001
Link the health probe 'probe-http' in each loadBalancingRule via the 'probe' property to enable health-based traffic routing
Consider enabling zone redundancy on the SQL MI instance (requires zone-redundant or geo-zone-redundant backup storage)
Consider replacing or fronting the L4 Load Balancer with Application Gateway for WAF, SSL offload, and path-based routing
